summary: API/CLI/docs module ownership is explicit and matches current code: API owns gated server job mutation and linked-run finish sync; CLI owns local execution orchestration; docs/verifier own evidence only.
Dependency direction is correct: CLI consumes API contract/client responses; API finish service has no dependency on CLI internals.
Migration order is sound: boundary confirmation -> API helper/lifecycle stabilization -> CLI runtime/result stabilization -> docs/verification -> review/integration.
Diagrams match the current high-level contract/claim/ack/finish flow; optional loop heartbeat is outside the core contract/claim/finish diagram scope.
risks: API and CLI finish/ack metadata can still drift; keep REV-001 focused on shared contract shape.
Loop heartbeat behavior is present in CLI runtime but not shown in the minimal CLI diagram.
Docs claim readiness must remain tied to isolated verification logs, not board intent alone.
recommended_board_updates: ARCH-001 done: cross-module boundary accepted; API owns auth-gated lifecycle/job mutation/linked-run sync, CLI owns local contract/claim/execute/ack/finish orchestration, docs/verifier own evidence only.
No diagram edits required for ARCH-001; current diagrams match the high-level contract/claim/ack/finish flow, with loop heartbeat treated as CLI runtime detail.
Reviewer should explicitly check API/CLI finish and ack metadata drift plus default-off gate preservation.
needs_followup: REV-001 should explicitly check API/CLI finish and ack metadata drift plus default-off gate preservation.
